title: santolina chamaecyparissus var. valida
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus var. valida, also known as Gray Santolina or French Lavender, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small, yellow flowers. The medicinal properties of Santolina chamaecyparissus var. valida include its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, infections, and digestive issues. This plant offers several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and alleviating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Santolina chamaecyparissus var. valida can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to promote wound healing and soothe irritated skin.
